title: The River, the Gold, and a 1400-Year-Old Warning
_note: TTS pronunciation: Euphrates = 'You-FRAY-teez'. On-screen captions use proper spelling. AUDIO/COPYRIGHT: the ayah clip (47:18) currently pulls Sudais via the Quran.com API; per the content strategy this must be swapped to a VERIFIED free-license (CC0/Public Domain) recording before commercial publishing, or recited in-house. Verify name pronunciations with a short test render to keep ElevenLabs usage low.

segments.text: Fourteen hundred years ago, a man who could not read or write described something we are watching happen right now.
segments.caption: There is a wall... no. There is a river.

segments.text: The Prophet, peace be upon him, said: the Hour will not come until the Euphrates uncovers a mountain of gold.
segments.caption: Sahih al-Bukhari 7119

segments.text: And he warned: when that day comes, people will fight over it, and ninety-nine out of every hundred will die. Each one hoping that he is the one who survives.
segments.caption: Sahih Muslim 2894

segments.text: Then he gave a command across the centuries: whoever is there, let him not take a single thing from it.
segments.caption: Sahih al-Bukhari 7119

segments.text: Here is what is true today. The Euphrates, a river that has flowed through all of human history, is shrinking. Scientists who study it warn it could one day run dry.
segments.caption: Present-day context — not a religious source

segments.text: The gold has not been uncovered. The prophecy is about the day the river finally gives out. And the signs, the Quran says, have already begun to come.
segments.caption: Surah Muhammad 47:18

segments.text: A man in the desert, who never held a book, told us where to look. And we are still watching the water fall.
segments.caption: Ketabi Studio — sourced Islamic stories

sources: Sahih al-Bukhari 7119 — the Euphrates will uncover a mountain/treasure of gold
Sahih Muslim 2894 — same prophecy; people will fight, do not take from it
Surah Muhammad 47:18 — 'for its signs have already come'

Present-day context: the documented long-term decline of the Tigris-Euphrates basin (e.g., NASA GRACE satellite water-storage studies). This is modern background ONLY and is clearly separated from the religious sources. The script does NOT claim the prophecy is fulfilled — the gold has not been uncovered.
